children.title: <AXValue 0x7938e9b80> {value = error:-25212 type = kAXValueAXErrorType}
children.description:
ok, i’m following. if it worked before and now even PostToolUse restore doesn’t work, something fundamental changed, not timing.

we should narrow it fast instead of guessing.
￼
likely root cause (based on your diff)

this line changed:

children.title: <AXValue 0x7939382a0> {value = error:-25212 type = kAXValueAXErrorType}
children.description:
the issue

pane_active with -a is not what you think it is
	•	it gives “active pane per window”
	•	not “the pane the user was actually in”

so now you’re often saving:
	•	the claude pane itself
or
	•	some random active pane in another window

→ so restore appears “broken” because it’s restoring to the wrong place
￼
quick sanity check

run this after a permission fires:

children.title: <AXValue 0x79393b4a0> {value = error:-25212 type = kAXValueAXErrorType}
children.description:
if they’re the same → you just saved the claude pane, not the previous pane
￼
why your old version worked

this:

children.title: <AXValue 0x793938580> {value = error:-25212 type = kAXValueAXErrorType}
children.description:
works because:
	•	it operates on the current window only
	•	which was the user’s window at that moment

so it correctly captured “where the user was”
￼
fix (minimal, don’t change your system)

revert just this part:

change PermissionRequest back to:

children.title: <AXValue 0x793938b80> {value = error:-25212 type = kAXValueAXErrorType}
children.description:
￼
but keep your new improvements

so final logic is:
	•	store:
	•	claude_self (full target) → for styling + focus
	•	claude_prev (pane_id only) → for restore

then restore:

children.title: <AXValue 0x7939393c0> {value = error:-25212 type = kAXValueAXErrorType}
children.description:
￼
why this works better
	•	pane ids (%123) are globally unique
	•	tmux can jump to them directly
	•	no need for session/window parsing
	•	avoids the broken -a semantics
￼
tl;dr

you broke it here:

-a changed the meaning of “active pane”

revert that line and it should work immediately again.
￼
if that doesn’t fix it, next step is to print both values side by side and see exactly what’s being captured.
